bound.note: the derived built-step 1.0849525 ms reproduces the GO read EXACTLY (resid 0.0e+00); land's stated ~1.085 (3 d.p.) reproduces 520.93 TPS -- a 0.023 TPS rounding bound. The derived step MATCHES land's stated built-step (step resid 4.7e-05 ms).

anchor_confirmation.note: E[T]_both=4.512 priced at the re-anchored built-step 1.0850 ms -> 520.9527 TPS == land's GO read 520.95 (CONFIRMED-520.95). Priced at the SERVED step 1.2182 ms it would be 463.97 (MISSES 500) -- the wrong denominator for tree decode.

floor_reread.note: the #241 floor = 4.512*(500/520.95) is a property of the ANCHOR (4.512, 520.95) and the 500 target ONLY: the slope 520.95/4.512 = 115.459 is pinned by the anchor, INDEPENDENT of how it factors into K_cal*tau/step. Re-derived directly under the built step it is 4.330527 -- it MOVES 0.00e+00 (< 1e-03) from #241's 4.330527: SURVIVES.

served_step_floor_vacuous.note: under the SERVED step 1.2182 the E[T] needed to clear 500 is 4.8624 > the 4.512 projection (VACUOUS: the build would miss even at projection) -- which is exactly why land's tree build must be priced at its own (built) step 1.085, under which the floor is a REAL 4.02% tolerance.

handoff_lines.land_245_fern_238_denken_241: land #245 + fern #238 card + denken #241 floor: the launch TPS gate should be read against the BUILT-step (1.0850 ms, tree decode), NOT the served-step 1.2182 (linear MTP K=7); under it E[T]_both=4.512 maps to reanchored_tps_at_ET4512=520.9527, so the 520.95 GO-read anchor is CONFIRMED (distinct-steps-both-valid: served 1.2182 and built 1.085 price two different paths, resid 0.0e+00) and my #241 4.3305 floor SURVIVES (moves 0.00e+00, slope-invariant) -- land's one build run's pass/fail line is read against a CONFIRMED step (measured-step is land #245's to read live).

honest_band.a_anchor_is_self_consistent_not_independ: the re-anchored built-step is DERIVED from land's GO read 520.95 (step_built = K_cal*4.512*tau/520.95), so reanchored_tps round-trips to 520.95 BY CONSTRUCTION. The CONFIRMATION is not the round-trip -- it is that the derived built-step 1.084953 ms == land's INDEPENDENTLY-stated effective built-step ~1.085 (resid 4.7e-05 ms) AND == denken #241 step_eff_landread 1.084953. The TRUE open question -- does land's LIVE build MEASURE a ~1.085 ms step -- stays land #245's to measure; this leg de-risks the ANALYTIC anchor.
honest_band.b_two_paths_not_one_step: the served 481.53 (linear MTP K=7, 1.2182 ms/step) and the built 520.95 (tree decode, 1.085 ms/step) are TWO decode paths with TWO per-step costs (10.9% apart). The composition prices each path at its OWN step; the 463.97 'inconsistency' denken #241 flagged was pricing the BUILT path's E[T] at the SERVED step -- a path-mismatch. The anchor is NOT optimistic; it is the tree path priced at the tree step.
honest_band.c_edge_is_step_cost_not_acceptance: the 520.95/481.53 = 1.0819 edge decomposes EXACTLY into step-ratio 1.1228 (built step cheaper) * E[T]-ratio 0.9635 (built E[T] LOWER than served's realized ~4.68). The win is a per-step-cost win from tree decode, partly OFFSET by the conservative both-bugs E[T] projection -- it does not assume more accepted tokens than the served path realizes.
honest_band.d_floor_is_anchor_property_slope_invaria: denken #241's 4.3305 floor is a property of the anchor (4.512, 520.95) and the 500 target ONLY -- the slope is pinned by the anchor, independent of the step decomposition. Re-derived directly under the built step it moves 0.00e+00 (< 1e-03): SURVIVES. Under the SERVED step the floor would be 4.8624 > 4.512 (vacuous), confirming the build must be read against the built step.

step_disambiguation.verdict_note: step_served (1.2182, linear MTP K=7) and step_built (1.0850, tree decode) are DISTINCT (10.9% apart): the tree-decode build legitimately runs a FASTER per-step cost, so the 520.95 anchor is NOT inconsistent with the banked 1.2182 -- it prices a DIFFERENT path. The 463.97 'inconsistency' was the artifact of pricing the BUILT E[T]=4.512 at the SERVED step.

synthesis.verdict: COMPOSITION STEP RE-ANCHORED -> DISTINCT-STEPS-BOTH-VALID. The served step (1.2182 ms, linear MTP K=7) and the built step (1.0850 ms, tree decode) are DISTINCT (10.9% apart): the tree-decode build legitimately runs a cheaper per-step cost, so the 520.95 GO-read anchor is NOT inconsistent with the banked 1.2182 -- it prices a DIFFERENT path. Re-deriving the built-step from land's reset trace gives 1.084953 ms (== denken #241 step_eff_landread, == land's stated ~1.085), under which E[T]_both=4.512 -> 520.9527 TPS == the GO read (CONFIRMED, resid 0.0e+00). The 463.97 'inconsistency' denken #241 flagged was pricing the BUILT E[T]=4.512 at the SERVED step. denken #241's 4.3305 floor is anchor-pinned (slope-invariant) and SURVIVES the re-anchor (moves 0.00e+00). The launch TPS gate is read against the BUILT step 1.085, NOT the served 1.2182. BASELINE stays 481.53. NOT a launch.

verdict_table.note: the launch TPS gate is read against the BUILT step (1.085, tree decode), NOT the served 1.2182 (linear MTP K=7). E[T]_both=4.512 is the BUILT path's projection; priced at its own step it -> 520.95 (CLEARS 500). Pricing it at the served step (463.97) is a path-mismatch, not a real shortfall.
